title: poa secunda var. incurva
intro: Poa secunda var. incurva, also known as blue grama, is a type of grass found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its distinctive blue-green color and slender leaves. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Poa secunda var. incurva include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used topically to promote healing and soothe irritated skin.
